title: Cued Recall of Paired Associates with Open-Loop Stimulation at Encoding or Retrieval
description: This dataset comprises intracranial electrophysiological recordings and behavioral data from a paired associates memory task with open-loop electrical brain stimulation applied during encoding or retrieval phases. Participants studied word pairs, performed distractor arithmetic tasks, and completed cued recall tests, with stimulation delivered to hippocampal and entorhinal cortex electrodes on randomly assigned lists. The dataset enables investigation of how targeted electrical stimulation affects memory encoding and retrieval processes.
methods_description: Participants performed a paired associates learning task consisting of 25 lists per session with the structure: encoding (6 word pairs, 4000 ms each), distractor (arithmetic problems), and cued recall (5000 ms per response). Open-loop electrical stimulation was applied to single electrodes in hippocampus and entorhinal cortex on 20 of 25 lists (10 encoding stimulation, 10 retrieval stimulation). Stimulation parameters included amplitude, pulse frequency, pulse width, and pulse count. Intracranial EEG recordings were acquired using Blackrock (250 nV units) or Medtronic (0.1 µV units) systems with monopolar or bipolar referencing schemes.
